title: Ports, Immutable Domain Values, Promise Ownership, and Lifecycle · Contract Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for ports, immutable domain values, promise ownership, and lifecycle and accepts only runtime and compiler evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, Node, network, XML, PostgreSQL, handle, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for explicitly owned TypeScript service graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Direct domain, application, fetch, parser, repository, scheduler, HTTP, and presentation dependencies through narrow typed ports.
Represent admitted identifiers, instants, URLs, states, and results as readonly values created only after runtime checks.
Assign every Promise a caller, awaited settlement, cancellation policy, error path, concurrency budget, and shutdown consequence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
